---
title: "LRP58: Mechanism model — letter-sound knowledge (L) -> word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP58 estimates the adjusted association $f^L$ between letter-sound knowledge and word reading,
using all three phase transitions.

::: {.callout-warning title="Residual boundary divergence — read the curve accordingly"}
This model keeps the nonparametric **HSGP** mechanism curve, which is the deliverable here (the
shape of the letter-sound → reading relationship). At reporting tier, with `target_accept` lifted to
0.999, a **single boundary divergence** remains on an otherwise-healthy posterior (R-hat = 1.00, min
ESS ≈ 2400). The HSGP geometry is the source — the vocabulary-predictor mechanisms
([LRP56](../lrp-rli-mech-056)/[LRP57](../lrp-rli-mech-057)) show the same pathology more strongly and
were entered linearly to resolve it. Here the curve is retained deliberately, so the model does
**not** strictly clear the zero-divergence gate; the one divergence is disclosed rather than removed
by discarding the curve or dropping a DAG-required adjuster. Read the curve as sound but not
gate-clean, and treat any feature localised near a sampling boundary with corresponding caution.
:::

## Adjustment set

Observed parents of $L$ (LS) under the revised DAG (#245): $\{A, HS, IG, IS, SP\}$.
The revision drops $EV \to LS$ (speech $SP$ is now the code cause), so the old
$E$/$R$ adjusters are **removed** — and conditioning on $E$, a common effect of
$SP$ and $RW$ that both reach $W$, would in fact **open a collider path**.

- $G$ — group ($IG$); always conditioned on via $\beta_G$.
- $A$ — age; conditioned on linearly by default ($\gamma_A\,z(\text{age})$).
- $HS$ (`hs` / `hs_missing`) — hearing, a new common cause of letter sounds. **New (#245).**
- $IS$ (`attend`) — intervention sessions, a common cause of $L$ and $W$. **New (#245).**
- $SP$ (`deapp_c`) — speech production, now a cause of letter-sound knowledge. **New (#245).**
- $W_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline word reading.

::: {.callout-important title="DAG decision: B is NOT in the adjustment set"}

Phoneme blending $B$ is a descendant of $L$ in the literacy-development cascade
($L \to \cdots \to B$) and should **not** be conditioned on: doing so would
change the estimand toward a direct-only component and can introduce collider
bias. The same argument excludes $F$ (CELF) and $P$ (phonetic spelling) from the
adjustment set.

This is the single most sensitive modelling decision in the mechanism-model
set. If a reviewer believes $B$ is an independent cause of $W$ not mediated
through $L$, they should fit the alternative (conditioning on $B$) and
compare $f^L$ estimates.

:::

### Interpretation caveats

- NUCA for $(L_{\text{post}}, W_{\text{post}})$ — not testable.
- Measurement error in $L_{\text{post}}$ attenuates $f^L$.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
